name: Clear Thought MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing structured reasoning and mental model frameworks to AI agents. Implements various thinking frameworks including first principles reasoning, second-order thinking, probabilistic reasoning, and other cognitive tools — helping AI agents reason more systematically and avoid common cognitive biases.
use_cases: Applying structured mental models to problem-solving from reasoning agents
Using first principles analysis for complex decisions from strategy agents
Improving AI agent decision quality with cognitive frameworks from analytical agents
Supporting systematic thinking for research and analysis workflows
not_for: Simple factual lookup tasks that don't require structured reasoning
Real-time decisions where reasoning overhead is unacceptable
Teams preferring their own custom reasoning frameworks
best_when: An AI agent tackles ambiguous, complex problems requiring structured analytical thinking — using mental model frameworks to improve reasoning quality and avoid hasty conclusions.
avoid_when: Your tasks are well-defined and straightforward. Structured reasoning adds overhead — evaluate whether the reasoning framework actually improves outcomes for your use case.

auth.notes: No authentication — local reasoning framework tool.

pricing.notes: Free open source MCP reasoning server.

known_agent_gotchas: Reasoning framework quality depends on LLM following the framework — not guaranteed
Mental model overhead can increase token usage significantly for complex reasoning chains
Community tool — evaluate whether built-in LLM reasoning is sufficient before adding dependency
Framework selection matters — agents need guidance on which mental model to apply when
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local reasoning tool. No credentials. No external calls. Low security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool. No credentials. No external data. Low risk.
